admission_type: open access with entry requirements evaluation

academic_requirements.summary: Admission is conditional on the candidate's academic background; a degree in Economics or related subjects is a preferential title. Candidates must hold both a bachelor's degree (or equivalent, obtained by 31 December 2026) and English at B2 level. Background is assessed from the transcript of records plus any additional academic documents, with a possible interview.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A three-year (bachelor's) degree or equivalent qualification obtained by 31 December 2026. Direct eligibility is granted to holders of an Italian three-year degree in classes L-18 or L-33 (DM 270/04) or L-17 or L-28 (DM 509/99) with a final grade of no less than 110/110.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Candidates who do not meet the direct-eligibility path (three-year degree + 110/110 in L-18/L-33/L-17/L-28 + B2 English) are admitted subject to passing an assessment of their knowledge and understanding in: economics and business studies; mathematical and statistical methods; legal principles and institutions.
academic_requirements.grade_threshold: Final bachelor's grade of at least 110/110 for the direct-eligibility (no-interview-needed) path in the listed economics degree classes; otherwise background is assessed and an interview may be required.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Applicants with a foreign academic qualification must apply exclusively via Dream Apply (apply.unimib.it).

language_requirements.required_level: English at B2 level of the CEFR.
accepted_proofs: Having passed, within the university career, one or more English-language exams totalling at least 5 CFU at B2 level.
Holding a B2-or-higher English certificate recognised by UNIMIB (Certificazioni_INGLESE_nov2025_UNIMIB list) obtained no more than 5 years ago.
Holding the UNIMIB 'Bbetween Languages' Open Badge - English at B2 or higher (UNIMIB students only).
Holding a bachelor's degree delivered entirely in English.
Holding a passport from a designated English-speaking country (e.g. Australia, Canada, Ireland, Malta, New Zealand, UK, USA, and others listed in the call).
Passing the in-person English proficiency test at UNIMIB (for candidates with an Italian qualification lacking the above).
language_requirements.minimum_scores: Per the UNIMIB accepted-certifications table (Academic Senate Regulation 216/2025/SA), B2 corresponds to: IELTS 5.5-6.5; TOEFL iBT 72-94; TOEFL ITP L+R+W 543-626 and S 58-63; Cambridge English Scale 160-179 (e.g. First/FCE); PTE Academic 59-75; Trinity ISE II; LanguageCert ESOL B2; Oxford Test of English 111-140.
language_requirements.exemption_rules: Candidates whose previous study qualifications were taught entirely in English, or who hold a passport from a designated English-speaking country, are considered to satisfy the requirement.
language_requirements.deadline_to_meet: For candidates with an Italian qualification the B2 requirement must be met by 7 September 2026; UNIMIB offers in-person test windows 22 June - 3 July 2026 (request by 16 June) and 2-7 September 2026 (request by 26 August).

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standardised written test (no TOLC/GRE/GMAT). Assessment of academic background via transcript review plus a possible online admission interview.
entry_exam_or_test.mandatory: An interview may be required to complete the background assessment; failing to attend the assigned interview prevents admission.
entry_exam_or_test.is_selective: Open access with entry requirements evaluation - the interview verifies adequacy of background rather than producing a competitive ranking.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Interview held online via Google Meet; the candidate receives a Google Meet invitation a few minutes before the scheduled time at the email given in the application.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Interview dates for 2026/27: 23 April 2026 (apply by 16 April), 21 May 2026 (apply by 14 May), 18 June 2026 (apply by 11 June), 23 July 2026 (apply by 16 July), 10 September 2026 (apply by 7 September), all 10:00 a.m. CET (apply by 2:00 p.m. CET on the listed dates).

rounds: A.Y. 2026/2027 (candidates with Italian qualifications, via Segreterie Online): applications open 1 April 2026 and close 7 September 2026, 2:00 p.m. CET, with rolling interview/application sub-deadlines tied to each interview date (16 April, 14 May, 11 June, 16 July, 7 September 2026).

For candidates with EU / non-EU-resident foreign qualifications applying via Dream Apply (apply.unimib.it), UNIMIB's 2026/27 master's calendar applies: 1st CALL 10 February - 31 March 2026; 2nd CALL 15 April - 6 July 2026; 3rd CALL 7 July - 7 September 2026 (the 3rd call is for EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Admitted candidates must enrol at the Online Register by 21 October 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Two tracks: Italian-qualification candidates apply via Segreterie Online (window 1 Apr - 7 Sep 2026); foreign-qualification candidates apply via Dream Apply following the UNIMIB unified master's calls.

rounds: A.Y. 2026/2027 for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (via Dream Apply, apply.unimib.it): 1st CALL 10 February - 31 March 2026; 2nd CALL 15 April - 6 July 2026. The 3rd CALL (7 July - 7 September 2026) is reserved for EU citizens and non-EU citizens already residing in Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Non-EU visa applicants must complete the English certification by 31 July 2026; these earlier deadlines ensure the documents needed for the Universitaly study-visa request are in place. Arrival in Italy for visa applicants and verification of original documents follow the UNIMIB international-admissions calendar (arrival by 31 January 2027; document verification by 28 February 2027).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Applicants with a foreign academic qualification must apply exclusively via Dream Apply; the bachelor's degree / entry requirements must be obtained by 31 December 2026.

required_documents.document_name: Valid ID document (identity card or passport)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded during the online application on Segreterie Online (Italian qualifications) or Dream Apply (foreign qualifications).
Bitmap or jpeg, resolution of at least 300x400 pixels; used for the university badge if enrolled.

required_documents.document_name: Self-certification of degree certificate (Dichiarazione Sostitutiva di Certificazione) or self-certification of university enrolment for graduating students
required_documents.notes: Italian-qualification candidates self-certify the qualification, graduation mark, passed exams, CFUs and SSD sectors. Graduating students declare exams passed/not yet taken and the expected graduation date.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency proof (B2 certificate / 5 CFU English exams / Open Badge / English-taught degree / qualifying passport / UNIMIB English test)
required_documents.notes: Certificate must be recognised by UNIMIB and obtained no more than 5 years ago; requirement met by 7 September 2026 for Italian-qualification candidates.

required_documents.document_name: Official transcripts, degree certificate / Diploma Supplement, and (for visa applicants) Declaration of Value / Statement of Comparability and passport copy
required_documents.notes: Foreign-qualification candidates apply exclusively via Dream Apply (apply.unimib.it) and upload the required academic documents there.
Implied by UNIMIB's standard master's career-evaluation fee; payable via the application platform.

source_quotes.quote: Degree programme language: English. Access: Open access with entry requirements evaluation.
LM-56 R | 2 years | Milan | English | open with entry requirements examination.

Admission is conditional on the candidate's academic background. A degree in Economics or related subjects is a preferential title ... three-year degree in the fields L-18 and L-33 ... or L-17 and L-28 ... with a grade of no less than 110/110.
They hold an English Language Certification of at least B2 level recognized by the University ... and obtained no more than 5 years ago. The requirement must be met by September 7th, 2026.
IELTS Academic / General Training B2: 5.5-6.5; TOEFL iBT B2: 72-94; TOEFL ITP B2: L. R. W.: 543-626 + S: 58-63.

Applications open on April 1th 2026 and close on September 7th 2026, 2.00 p.m. CET. The interview will be held online via Google Meet ... Admitted candidates must enrol at the Online Register by October 21st 2026.

Master's Programmes: 1st CALL February 10th, 2026 / March 31st, 2026; 2nd CALL April 15th, 2026 / July 6th, 2026; 3rd CALL July 7th, 2026 / September 7th, 2026 (for EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy).

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language confirmed fully English (official 2026/27 INTECO call PDF and en.unimib.it graduate page).

CFU/subject prerequisites confirmed qualitatively: direct eligibility for L-18/L-33/L-17/L-28 graduates with 110/110; otherwise an assessment of economics/business, mathematical-statistical and legal knowledge. The call does not state a numeric SECS-P CFU minimum, so the requirement is partly threshold-free (knowledge assessment).

B2 English confirmed; numeric IELTS/TOEFL scores are not in the INTECO call itself but in UNIMIB's central accepted-certifications table (Academic Senate Regulation 216/2025/SA), from which the B2 thresholds were taken.

EU and non-EU calls confirmed: Italian-qualification track via Segreterie Online opens 1 April - 7 September 2026; foreign-qualification / visa track via Dream Apply follows the unified 2026/27 master's calls (non-EU visa applicants limited to the 1st and 2nd calls).

Deadlines re-verified 2026-06-11 against apply.unimib.it 2026/27 calendar (Master track: 1st CALL 10 Feb - 31 Mar 2026; 2nd CALL 15 Apr - 6 Jul 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]; 3rd CALL 7 Jul - 7 Sep 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]). The previously inserted 4-call Nov 2025 - Sep 2026 schedule is the Bachelor/single-cycle calendar and was incorrect for this Master. Programme keeps its own Italian-qualification window (1 April - 7 September 2026 via Segreterie OnLine) per official INTECO 2026/27 call PDF.
